vol_02.context: Inside render() — called every rAF frame (typically 60x/second). The element is static and created once in setup().
vol_02.fix: In setup(), after appending statusEl to container, store the reference: this.statusEl = statusEl. In render(), replace document.getElementById call with this.statusEl. Declare private statusEl: HTMLElement | null = null as a class field.
vol_02.note: The setInterval at line 143-145 also calls document.getElementById('mg-volonte-timer-fill') and 'mg-volonte-timer' — those fire at 100ms intervals, which is acceptable but the same caching pattern applies for consistency.

run_02.cause: RUN-02 occurs at tier 1 (not tier 0). At tier 0, totalPairs=6 → totalTiles=12 → gridRows=ceil(12/4)=3 → 12 slots, 12 tiles: grid is full, no empty slots. At tier 1, totalPairs=7 → totalTiles=14 → gridRows=ceil(14/4)=4 → 16 slots, but only 14 tiles are placed (generateTiles breaks outer loop at placed>=14). The last row has 2 empty (unrendered) slots at positions [14] and [15] in the grid.
run_02.fix: The empty slots are cosmetic only — the canvas simply does not draw anything in those positions. No gameplay impact (click handlers use findIndex on this.tiles which has exactly 14 entries; out-of-bounds clicks return -1 and are ignored). If the visual gap is undesirable, center the last row by offsetting partial rows: compute lastRowCount = totalTiles % gridCols and add x-offset = (gridCols - lastRowCount) / 2 * (tileSize + tilePad) for the final row's tiles in generateTiles().

new_bugs.description: document.getElementById('mg-combat-status') called on every render() frame (~60x/second). The element is created once in setup() and never re-parented. This is the same pattern as VOL-02 in mg_volonte.ts.
new_bugs.fix: Cache the reference in setup() as a class field (e.g., this.statusEl: HTMLElement | null = null) and use it directly in render(). Also apply the same caching to 'mg-combat-timer-fill' and 'mg-combat-timer' in the setInterval at lines 147-148 for consistency, though those are lower priority at 100ms interval.

clean_files.notes: Score formula (hits/totalRounds)*100 is correct. ended guard in render() present (line 293 checks this.gameOver). endTimeout stored and cleared in cancelTimers(). advanceRound getElementById calls are in response to user events, not per-frame. No zombie rAF risk. No edge case in score calculation.

All getElementById calls are inside setInterval (100ms), not per-frame. Score formula timeInZone/totalElapsed uses +0.001 guard to prevent division-by-zero at game start. endGame() math is correct for all termination paths (timer expiry and potential future early-exit). Physics clamp at [-1,1] is correct. cancelTimers removes all 6 event listeners including pointercancel (C96).
